reviews.author: Diego A.
reviews.text: I prefer the counter seats at the smaller branches.

reviews.author: Emma R.
reviews.text: The spicy level 5 actually means it. Be warned.

reviews.author: Iris K.
reviews.text: Best late-night ramen in Shibuya. Open till 6am. Saved me after a missed last train more than once.

reviews.author: Diego A.
reviews.text: Booth feels claustrophobic if you are tall.

reviews.author: Lin G.
reviews.text: The way the curtain works — you only see the kitchen staff passing the bowl under. Pure theatre.

reviews.author: Akira S.
reviews.text: Customization is too much. Just give me a bowl.

reviews.author: Robert F.
reviews.text: Soft boiled egg is perfect. Custardy without being runny.

reviews.author: Carlos H.
reviews.text: The focus booth is genius. No eye contact, no small talk, just you and the tonkotsu. This is the future of eating.

reviews.author: Ben H.
reviews.text: Best late-night ramen in Shibuya. Open till 6am. Saved me after a missed last train more than once.

reviews.author: Maria L.
reviews.text: Customization sheet is overwhelming at first but you get the perfect bowl every time.

reviews.author: Lin G.
reviews.text: Tonkotsu broth is the real deal. Rich, deep, exactly what you want at 2am.

reviews.text: I live in Tokyo and go once a week. This is not a tourist recommendation, this is a local recommendation.

reviews.text: My booth neighbour kept talking on the phone. The whole point is silence.

reviews.text: Chashu is tender, broth is layered, noodles are the right texture. Hits all three marks.

reviews.author: Maria L.
reviews.text: Order from the vending machine, fill out the customization slip, sit down. Two minutes later your bowl arrives.

reviews.text: I have eaten ramen across Japan. This is in my top 3.

reviews.text: Did exactly what it said on the box. Tonkotsu, customization, solo booth.
